name: Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales from Arguedas
name_original: Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales desde Arguedas
region_intro: Road cycling around Valtierra offers access to the unique semi-desert landscape of the Bardenas Reales Natural Park, a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve. This region is characterized by wide plains, canyons, and plateaus formed from clay, chalk, and sandstone, with areas like La Blanca Baja showcasing dramatic eroded features. The terrain varies from the arid, sculpted formations of the Bardenas to greener, more rugged sections with small pine forests and higher hills in the south. Road cyclists can navigate routes that traverse these distinct natural environments, providing diverse riding experiences. Best road cycling routes around Valtierra The most popular road cycling route is Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales from Arguedas, an easy 18.7 miles (30.1 km) trail that takes 1 hour 31 minutes to complete. This route provides an introduction to the unique semi-desert landscape of the Bardenas Reales. Another top favourite among local road cyclists is Views of the Bardenas Reales — return via the Ebro River - from Alfaro, a moderate 66.8 miles (107.4 km) path. This route offers expansive views of the Bardenas Reales and follows the Ebro River. Local road cyclists also love the San Francisco Church – St. Michael's Collegiate Church loop from Alfaro, a 40.3 miles (64.9 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 2 hours 47 minutes. Road cycling around Valtierra is defined by the unique semi-desert landscape of Bardenas Reales, wide plains, and eroded clay formations. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes. The routes in Valtierra are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from 2 reviews. More than 400 road cyclists have used komoot to explore Valtierra's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many road cycling routes are available around Valtierra?
A: There are over 15 road cycling routes around Valtierra, offering a variety of experiences. These routes are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect when road cycling near Valtierra?
A: Road cycling around Valtierra primarily features the unique semi-desert landscape of the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. You'll encounter wide plains, canyons, and plateaus formed from clay, chalk, and sandstone, particularly in areas like La Blanca Baja. The terrain also includes greener, more rugged sections with small pine forests and higher hills in the south, offering diverse riding experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year for road cycling in Valtierra?
A: The best time for road cycling in Valtierra and the Bardenas Reales is generally between September and June. During these months, temperatures are more pleasant. It's advisable to avoid extremely hot summer days or periods of heavy rain, as conditions can become challenging.

Q: Are there road cycling routes suitable for beginners around Valtierra?
A: Yes, there are several road cycling routes suitable for beginners. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e986863866/parque-natural-de-las-bardenas-reales-from-arguedas" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Parque Natural de las Bardenas Reales from Arguedas&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 30.1 km trail that provides an excellent introduction to the unique semi-desert landscape with limited elevation gain.

Q: Are there challenging road cycling routes for experienced cyclists?
A: Absolutely. For experienced cyclists seeking a challenge, routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20151432" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cervera del Río Alhama – Cervera del Río Alhama loop from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt; offer a difficult 110.9 km ride with significant elevation changes, providing a demanding and rewarding experience.

Q: What natural landmarks or viewpoints can I see while road cycling near Valtierra?
A: While road cycling near Valtierra, you can encounter several striking natural landmarks and viewpoints within the Bardenas Reales Natural Park. Notable sights include the iconic 'chimney' rock formation of &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/945742" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castildetierra&lt;/a&gt;, and panoramic vistas from &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3927882" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardena Blanca Viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; or Cabezo de las Cortinillas. The entire &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/1170992" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bardenas Reales Natural Park&lt;/a&gt; itself is a UNESCO Biosphere Reserve, offering a unique 'lunar' landscape.

Q: Are there any historical sites or castles accessible by road bike?
A: Yes, beyond the natural wonders, you can also find historical sites. For instance, &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3848041" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñaflor Castle (also known as Doña Blanca Castle)&lt;/a&gt; is an interesting historical landmark that can be incorporated into your cycling routes, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Are there circular road cycling routes available?
A: Yes, circular routes are a popular option in the region. Many routes are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31476515" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;San Francisco Church – St. Michael's Collegiate Church loop from Alfaro&lt;/a&gt; is a moderate 64.9 km circular trail.

Q: What do other road cyclists enjoy most about road cycling in Valtierra?
A: The komoot community highly rates road cycling in Valtierra, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 400 cyclists. Reviewers often praise the unique semi-desert landscape of the Bardenas Reales, the wide-open spaces, and the distinct colors and eroded formations that create a memorable and surreal riding experience.

Q: Are there family-friendly road cycling routes around Valtierra?
A: While the Bardenas Reales offers many tracks, some unpaved, there are road cycling options that are generally easy and suitable for families. Routes with limited elevation gain and paved surfaces, such as the easy routes available, can be enjoyed by families looking for a less strenuous ride through the unique landscape.

Q: Is parking available for road cyclists near the routes in Valtierra?
A: Valtierra serves as an excellent base for exploring the Bardenas Reales, and the park itself is accessible by car. While specific parking areas for cyclists are not always explicitly signposted for every route, you can typically find parking in towns like Valtierra or Arguedas, which serve as common starting points for many cycling adventures into the park.

Q: Are there cafes or places to stop for refreshments along the road cycling routes?
A: While the Bardenas Reales Natural Park itself is a vast, semi-desert area with limited services, towns surrounding the park like Valtierra, Arguedas, or Alfaro offer cafes, restaurants, and shops where you can refuel before or after your ride. It's always a good idea to carry sufficient water and snacks, especially when venturing into the park's more remote sections.
tips: Stay on the "white" / gravel path from komoot (the one used by cars), and you won't be stuck in the mud mid-season.

Arguedas (Navarra), a charming place that transcends being a gateway to the Bardenas Reales:
The town center is welcoming, ideal for strolling through its streets and discovering historic buildings such as the Town Hall and the Casa Muruzábal Palace (16th-18th centuries).
Two chapels stand out: the Virgen del Yugo (Gothic-Renaissance style, 17th century, on a hill with panoramic views) and the Hermitage of San Miguel (16th century).
